mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid only when policy is moving from a restrictive to a neutralizing stance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if triggered easing episodes produce negative average returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a local threshold signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should depress risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising rather than flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing unemployment episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final demand and housing activity are expanding together, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not present when only one of the two series improves.
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform single-series up-moves, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-expansion instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in INDPRO marks an industrial contraction that weakens earnings momentum and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when the decline is isolated to temporary noise without persistence.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the signal loses predictive value when the decline is not followed by a second monthly contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A housing trough followed by a rebound in HOUST signals stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and a better growth outlook, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest after prior housing weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST turns up after at least 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound episodes fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the success rate is not above the unconditional base rate in post-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is still below the implied inflation pressure, real policy remains accommodative and equities should rise over the next week. The signal requires inflation to be rising faster than policy rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
regime_hint: inflation surprise with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration days without a policy response do not yield positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition has no edge over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series together with a falling UNRATE signals labor-market strengthening that supports consumer income and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is valid only when both employment and unemployment move in the expected directions.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls in the same monthly observation
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll gains and unemployment declines do not outperform either series alone, or if forward 20-day SPX returns are not above baseline in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A broad slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS together signals weakening final demand that should weigh on earnings revisions and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relationship breaks if GDP weakens while retail sales remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears when one series is excluded.
